authority: repository-local docs, skill-mirror, policy, dependency, test, Git commit, main push, and remote-ref readback only; no provider or external-runtime access or mutation

checks.result: check:docs passed 204 selected files and wrote a zero-finding JSON receipt; negative fixtures cover metadata, links, inventories, lifecycle, commands, package README ownership, successor/tombstone routing, portability, semantic claim paraphrases, accepted-task states, and occurrence-control weakening.

check:skills passed 164 instruction/reference files and 15 mirrors; package-structure and prd-review replace the broken create-package route, YAML plus Effect Schema validates skill/UI metadata, all local TypeScript examples are inspected, and negative mirror/reference/portability/example fixtures pass.

The evaluator rejected finite phrase matching plus weak YAML/example coverage. Structured claim types, semantic term groups, non-claim terms, accepted-task comparison, Schema-decoded YAML, all-local-skill example scanning, and paraphrase fixtures then received strict PASS.
The accepted HGI-308 record is mechanically required and 24 focused boundary tests retain exact matching, duplicate rejection, occurrence isolation, and stale-entry detection.

Effect setup, boundary/docs/skill audits, 38 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint fixtures, Knip, five typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with schema-valid synthetic Executor test configuration and no provider call.
The accepted semantic commit was pushed; after fetch, local HEAD and origin/main both resolved to aa61e36a19248122efb3a8461252b1dd036f69e8.

postcondition: Bundjil mechanically rejects documentation, current-owner, command, lifecycle, skill-mirror, metadata, reference, portability, and forbidden-example drift with bounded owner-aware receipts; HGI-310 may build the local docs-maintainer skill on this enforced baseline.
rollback: Revert the validation closeout, then semantic aa61e36a19248122efb3a8461252b1dd036f69e8; preserve HGI-300/HGI-301/HGI-308 and the accepted occurrence-control decision.
limitations: Repository structure and semantic policy do not prove provider, workflow, deployment, credential, release, or application-runtime actuality.
Semantic claim vocabulary is an explicit maintained control; a new claim domain requires an owner-reviewed rule and positive/negative fixtures.
The semantic commit is unsigned because the configured 1Password signing agent remained unavailable; content, tree, ancestry, push, and remote identity are verified.
nonClaims: No provider, deployment, workflow, GitHub setting, secret, webhook, message, credential, production, release, or publication system was read or changed.
Passing docs and skill checks does not grant external operation authority or prove runtime behavior.
